name: dilated_h64_0_90
description: The 0.9001 contest-CUDA baseline (2026-04-25). Dilated h=64 renderer + matched poses + AV1 CRF=50 masks. Renderer + poses + masks are a TRIPLE JOINT ARTIFACT — see lane_b_post_mortem and project_baseline_poses_load_bearing memory entries. Modifying any of the three breaks the others.

auth_score.rate_unscaled_bytes_ratio_note: Council R2 caught a reporting ambiguity: auth_eval_renderer prints 'Rate: X' as the unscaled bytes-ratio (= archive_bytes/gt_bytes). The score CONTRIBUTION is 25 * that. Always quote the contribution in tables to avoid confusion.
auth_score.gpu_model: see council R2 finding R2-5: gpu_model not recorded at original measurement (2026-04-25). On re-eval, capture from `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader`. Different CUDA archs (A100 vs T4 vs 4090) have slightly different fp16 numerics — record this on every auth measurement going forward.

stats.note: LARGE-VALUED FiLM modulation coefficients, NOT raw frame-pair PoseNet outputs (which would be ~0±1). Confirmed load-bearing 2026-04-26: real vs zero poses → 33% pixel shift, max=105 on 0-255 range. Renderer + poses are JOINT artifact. Pose TTO from extract_gt_pose_targets() init causes 23x PoseNet degradation (LANE-B 2026-04-26).

masks.mkv.note: Council R2-1 finding: previously claimed 'regenerable from upstream' but no proof script existed. Now committed (411KB) so the baseline is byte-reproducible. Re-encoding with different ffmpeg/svtav1 versions WILL drift the score (renderer + poses + masks are a TRIPLE joint artifact).

smoke_command: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && .venv/bin/python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'src'); sys.path.insert(0, 'upstream'); from tac.renderer_export import load_asymmetric_checkpoint; from tac.submission_archive import load_optimized_poses; r = load_asymmetric_checkpoint(open('submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/renderer.bin','rb').read(), device='cpu'); p = load_optimized_poses('submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/optimized_poses.bin', expected_n_pairs=600); assert tuple(p.shape)==(600,6), p.shape; print('OK: renderer loads, poses (600,6), shapes valid')"
auth_reproduction_command: On a CUDA machine: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && .venv/bin/python experiments/auth_eval_renderer.py --checkpoint submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/renderer.bin --upstream-dir upstream --device cuda --poses submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/optimized_poses.bin --archive-size-bytes $(python -c 'import zipfile,os; z=zipfile.ZipFile("/tmp/check.zip","w",zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED,9); [z.write(f"submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/{n}",arcname=n) for n in ("renderer.bin","masks.mkv","optimized_poses.bin")]; z.close(); print(os.path.getsize("/tmp/check.zip"))')

provenance.reason: User invoked durability-guarantee on baseline poses after LANE-B (2026-04-26) wasted 6.5h initializing TTO from the wrong pose distribution. Original location was untracked (experiments/results/ is gitignored). R2 also added masks.mkv (was not committed) + full SHA256s (R1 only had 16-char prefixes).

lane_b_post_mortem.what_lane_b_did_wrong: Initialized init_poses = extract_gt_pose_targets(gt_frames, posenet) — frame-pair PoseNet outputs, distribution ~0±1. The renderer expects FiLM conditioning in the LARGE distribution shown above (mean 5.2, max 35). The 1000-step Adam couldn't recover from the orders-of-magnitude wrong init.
lane_b_post_mortem.what_lane_b_should_have_done: Pass --gt-poses-path submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/optimized_poses.bin to the bootstrap. optimize_poses.py line 690-692 honors this and uses the canonical poses as warm start.
lane_b_post_mortem.council_r2_extension: The renderer+poses+masks form a TRIPLE joint artifact. LANE-A (rate attack via mask re-encoding) MUST measure the full archive auth score, not just `25 * new_archive_bytes / gt_bytes` arithmetic — re-encoding masks at higher CRF will likely drift PoseNet+SegNet non-linearly because the renderer was trained against CRF=50 mask statistics.
